body.title: agent_datacenter: Igor shim — subprocess launch, exit-42, tmux integration
body.description:
IgorShim owns Igor's lifecycle: subprocess launch via the igor bash launcher, exit-42 awareness, tmux session integration.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/devices/igor/shim.py:
  class IgorShim(BaseShim):
    device_id = 'igor-wild-0001'  # from IGOR_INSTANCE_NAME env var
    start() → if IGOR_TMUX_SESSION set: tmux new-window -t {session} -n igor 'igor'; else subprocess.Popen(['igor'])
    stop() → SIGTERM to igor process; wait up to 30s for clean shutdown
    restart() → stop(); start() — skeleton uses this for normal restarts
    self_test() → send 'rack.ping' to igor via comms://igor-wild-0001; expect 'rack.pong' within 5s
    rollback() → SIGKILL igor process if start() times out
  Exit-42 awareness: igor shim recognizes exit code 42 = intentional restart (not a failure)
    record_failure() not called for exit-42 restarts — they don't count toward auto-block threshold
    All other non-zero exits count as failures

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/igor/shim.py

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: comment explaining exit-42 semantics (intentional restart, not failure)

Scope boundary:
IN: start/stop/restart/self_test/rollback, exit-42 awareness, tmux integration
OUT: manual-only block enforcement (T-adc-igor-block-manual-only)

Test plan:
- IgorShim.device_id == 'igor-wild-0001' (from env)
- Exit code 42 does NOT trigger record_failure()
- Exit code 1 DOES trigger record_failure()
- self_test() returns {passed: False, details: 'igor not responding'} when igor is down (no exception)
